#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <numeric>
#include <algorithm>
#include <iomanip>

// Function to compute Z-array for a string
std::vector<int> compute_z_array(const std::string& s) {
    int n = s.length();
    std::vector<int> z(n);
    z[0] = n; // Conventionally, z[0] is n
    int l = 0, r = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i <= r) {
            z[i] = std::min(r - i + 1, z[i - l]);
        }
        while (i + z[i] < n && s[z[i]] == s[i + z[i]]) {
            ++z[i];
        }
        if (i + z[i] - 1 > r) {
            l = i;
            r = i + z[i] - 1;
        }
    }
    return z;
}

int main() {
    std::ios_base::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(NULL);

    std::string s;
    std::cin >> s;
    int n = s.length();

    // Precompute Z-arrays for all cyclic shifts of s
    // z_arrays[i] stores the Z-array for s[i..N-1] + s[0..i-1]
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> z_arrays(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        std::string shifted_s = s.substr(i) + s.substr(0, i);
        z_arrays[i] = compute_z_array(shifted_s);
    }

    // Group indices by their character s[i]
    std::vector<std::vector<int>> char_indices(26);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        char_indices[s[i] - 'a'].push_back(i);
    }

    double total_win_prob = 0.0;

    // Iterate over each possible initial shift k (Kolya's choice)
    for (int k = 0; k < n; ++k) {
        char first_char = s[k]; // t[0] = s[k]
        const std::vector<int>& possible_ks_after_first_char = char_indices[first_char - 'a'];

        if (possible_ks_after_first_char.size() == 1) {
            // If only one k matches t[0], Vasya wins immediately.
            total_win_prob += 1.0;
            continue;
        }

        // Vasya needs to choose an optimal position p to query t[p]
        // to maximize his win probability for this specific k.
        // This means finding a p that uniquely identifies k among possible_ks_after_first_char.
        
        // Vasya wants to find a p such that for all k' in possible_ks_after_first_char, k' != k,
        // s_k[p] != s_k'[p].
        // This is equivalent to s[(k+p)%N] != s[(k'+p)%N].
        // Or, s[(k+p)%N] != s[(k'+p)%N] for all k' in possible_ks_after_first_char, k' != k.

        // For a fixed k (Kolya's choice), Vasya wants to find a p (1 to N-1)
        // that distinguishes k from all other k' in possible_ks_after_first_char.
        // If such a p exists, Vasya wins for this k.
        // If no such p exists, Vasya loses for this k.

        bool can_win_for_this_k = false;
        for (int p = 1; p < n; ++p) { // Vasya chooses p from 1 to N-1
            char char_at_p = s[(k + p) % n]; // t[p] = s_k[p]
            bool p_distinguishes_k = true;

            for (int k_prime : possible_ks_after_first_char) {
                if (k_prime == k) continue;

                // Check if s_k_prime[p] is different from s_k[p]
                if (s[(k_prime + p) % n] == char_at_p) {
                    // If s_k_prime[p] is the same as s_k[p], then this p does not distinguish k from k_prime.
                    // Vasya cannot uniquely determine k with this p.
                    p_distinguishes_k = false;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (p_distinguishes_k) {
                // If this p distinguishes k from all other k_prime, Vasya can win for this k.
                can_win_for_this_k = true;
                break; // Vasya found an optimal p, no need to check other p's for this k.
            }
        }

        if (can_win_for_this_k) {
            total_win_prob += 1.0;
        }
    }

    std::cout << std::fixed << std::setprecision(15) << total_win_prob / n << std::endl;

    return 0;
}

For each `k`, it determines the set of `k'` values that would produce the same `t[0]` as `k`.
If this set contains only `k` itself, Vasya wins immediately.
Otherwise, Vasya needs to choose a position `p` (from 1 to `N-1`) to query `t[p]`.
Vasya wins for this `k` if there exists *any* `p` such that `t[p]` (which is `s[(k+p)%N]`)
